title: Update Database Schema Page with enhanced card layout (description, PK/FK labels)

requirements.summary: Update DatabaseSchemaPage.tsx to display 10 table cards with enhanced content including table name, description, field list, and PK/FK labels using hardcoded JSX
acceptanceCriteria: All 10 table cards are rendered: Users, Doctors, Specialties, Doctor_Schedules, Appointments, Payments, Reviews, Medical_Records, Corporate_Accounts, Subscriptions.
Each card displays the table name as a bold heading.
Each card displays a single-line description of the table's purpose.
Each card lists all fields in 'Column Name – Data Type' format.
The Primary Key field on each card is clearly labelled with a 'PK' indicator.
All Foreign Key fields on each card are clearly labelled with a 'FK' indicator and the referenced table name.
No .map(), conditional rendering, hooks (useState, useEffect, useRef, etc.), or external library imports beyond React and Tailwind are present in the file.
All content is hardcoded JSX — no dynamic rendering or computed values.
Styling uses Tailwind utility classes consistent with the Medical Blue theme used elsewhere in the application.
The component is a single default-export functional component with no props.

file_operations.description: Replace the existing DatabaseSchemaPage component with a hardcoded JSX implementation that renders exactly 10 table cards (Users, Doctors, Specialties, Doctor_Schedules, Appointments, Payments, Reviews, Medical_Records, Corporate_Accounts, Subscriptions). Each card must display: (1) table name as a bold heading, (2) a one-line plain-English description of the table's purpose, (3) a field list where each row shows 'Column Name – Data Type', (4) the Primary Key field clearly labelled with a 'PK' badge (e.g., inline span with Tailwind classes like 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800 px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-xs font-semibold'), and (5) any Foreign Key fields clearly labelled with a 'FK' badge and the referenced table name (e.g., 'FK → TableName'). Remove all .map() calls, hooks (useState, useEffect, useRef), conditional rendering logic, and external library imports beyond React. Use only Tailwind utility classes for styling, consistent with the Medical Blue theme (using colors like blue-50, blue-600, blue-100, gray-700, etc.). Maintain the component as a single default-export functional component with no props, no routing logic, no backend calls, and no animations. Ensure all content is hardcoded JSX with no dynamic rendering or computed values.
